# modules/site-packages/

Emio Labs is distributed with its own Python environment. If you need to install additional Python packages for your lab, you can do so in the `modules/site-packages` directory of your lab. It will be added to the `PYTHONPATH` when using the `python-button` or `runsofa-button`, allowing you to import the installed packages in your Python code.

To install packages to this directory, you can use the `#python-button` in the `.md` file of your lab. This button allows you to run a Python command in the terminal (using the Python environment of Emio Labs). Use this button to specify the command to install packages using pip.

For example, if you want to install the `dummy_test` package, you can add the following line to a `requirements.txt` file:

```
dummy_test==0.1.3
```

Then add the following line to your `.md` file:

```markdown
#python-button("-m pip install --target 'assets/labs/yourlab/modules/site-packages' -r assets/labs/yourlab/requirements.txt")
```

Click the following button, and check the directory `assets/labs/lab_empty/modules/site-packages` to see the installed package.  

#python-button("-m pip install --target 'assets/labs/lab_empty/modules/site-packages' -r assets/labs/lab_empty/requirements.txt")
